jokes.text: Oh, I'm trying to see how close I can get to your head without hitting it.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what Will is doing (presumably throwing grapes)
jokes.punchline: Will's mock-serious explanation of his terrible aim as intentional precision
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith deflection humor - turning incompetence into mock sophistication with perfect delivery timing

jokes.text: You know, you're not doing a very good job.
jokes.setup: Will's claim of precision grape-throwing
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dry observation that he's clearly missing
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective straight-man response that grounds Will's absurdity

jokes.text: Now you're in trouble sequence and kiss
jokes.setup: Lisa threatens Will about throwing grapes
jokes.punchline: The 'trouble' turns out to be romantic rather than violent
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom misdirection from conflict to romance, executed competently

jokes.text: Oh, you mean like in a suave, debonair, Denzel-ian kind of way, right?
jokes.setup: Lisa calls Will 'goofy'
jokes.punchline: Will desperately reframes 'goofy' as sophisticated by invoking Denzel Washington
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - the invented word 'Denzel-ian' and desperate spin on criticism shows his ego and charm

jokes.text: Your mom goofy.
jokes.setup: Will's wounded pride from being called goofy
jokes.punchline: Childish comeback muttered after Lisa leaves
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly childish moment that shows Will's immaturity while being perfectly timed and economical

jokes.text: My horoscope said that I will prosper in my house. So I figured I better go buy one.
jokes.setup: Hilary announces she has news
jokes.punchline: She takes horoscope literally and decides to buy a house based on it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - shallow materialism disguised as cosmic destiny with flawless character integration

jokes.text: Nothing fancy. No more than six or seven bedrooms. And I don't even care if it has a wine cellar. For now.
jokes.setup: Claims she wants nothing fancy
jokes.punchline: Immediately describes an extremely fancy house, with escalating qualifier
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary contradiction with perfect escalation through 'For now' - shows her complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: I have discovered a unique method of savings. I never spend my own money.
jokes.setup: How will she afford such an expensive house
jokes.punchline: Her 'savings method' is just spending other people's money
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic perfectly delivered - the mock-serious tone of 'unique method' makes the punchline land harder

jokes.text: All this time, you've been hoarding your salary while feasting away on the family fortune?
jokes.setup: Hilary's revelation about her spending habits
jokes.punchline: Carlton's incredulous summary using 'feasting' imagery
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction. 'Feasting away' is colorful language that fits Carlton's verbose style.

jokes.text: All I heard is that she's moving.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what Phil thinks about Hilary's financial parasitism
jokes.punchline: Phil focuses only on the positive aspect - her leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect dad joke - the selective hearing and deadpan delivery make this a standout reaction beat

jokes.text: CARLTON: Freeloader. PHIL: Undependable. ASHLEY: He's not a freeloader. CARLTON: Lazy.
jokes.setup: Will asks for one word to describe him
jokes.punchline: Family immediately piles on with multiple negative descriptors
jokes.explanation: Great ensemble moment with rapid-fire insults and Ashley's failed defense making it funnier

jokes.text: ALL: Yes, that's it. CARLTON: Exactly. We're going to go with goofy, Will.
jokes.setup: Will mentions Lisa called him goofy
jokes.punchline: Unanimous immediate agreement from the family
jokes.explanation: Solid callback with perfect timing - Carlton's formal declaration seals the family consensus

jokes.text: Will, if there's one thing I know, it's about women not calling.
jokes.setup: Carlton gives Will relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Carlton's expertise is in being rejected, not success
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Carlton's oblivious self-burn delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Pfft. I mean, if I put my mind to it, I could write in any one of them old magazines.
jokes.setup: Lisa is excited about her friend Marvin being published
jokes.punchline: Will dismissively claims he could easily do the same thing
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Will's arc - his dismissive attitude and 'them old magazines' shows his ignorance

jokes.text: Pfft. I mean, if I put my mind to it, I could write in any one of them old magazines.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about friend being published in op-ed
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive confidence that writing is easy
jokes.explanation: Sets up Will's overconfidence that drives the episode. 'Them old magazines' is good character voice.

jokes.text: A bubble bath? 'Marvin, take me away.'
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions taking a bubble bath to read Marvin's article
jokes.punchline: Will's jealous fantasy of Lisa's romantic response to the writing
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's insecurity and competitive nature through jealous imagination

jokes.text: Oh, a coloring book.
jokes.setup: Will says he could write a book with pictures that real people would want to read
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately identifies this as a coloring book
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - Carlton's quick wit cuts through Will's pretensions with surgical precision

jokes.text: Well. / Well, what? / Never mind.
jokes.setup: Will's 'write good' grammar error
jokes.punchline: Carlton starting to correct him, then giving up
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Carlton's exasperation. The truncated correction is funnier than completing it.

jokes.text: I'm gonna call it Celebrities' Houses At Night.
jokes.setup: Will announces his book title
jokes.punchline: The title itself is hilariously specific and pointless
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept that becomes funnier each time it's repeated - the specificity of 'At Night' is perfect

jokes.text: Hey, man, look, I even trim my nose hair. Check it out.
jokes.setup: Will listing his grooming improvements for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Proudly showing off trimmed nose hair as romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works because of Will's pride in basic grooming

jokes.text: Will's romantic speech about Lisa
jokes.setup: Will gets genuinely romantic describing his feelings for Lisa
jokes.punchline: The contrast with his usual goofiness and Carlton's emotional response
jokes.explanation: Sets up Carlton's emotional response while showing Will's genuine depth

jokes.text: Carlton crying and asking for a hug, then driving erratically
jokes.setup: Will's heartfelt speech about love
jokes.punchline: Carlton gets so emotional he cries and nearly crashes the car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his extreme emotional reaction creates physical danger in classic sitcom escalation

jokes.text: Look like Bob up in there getting his freak on. Bobby, come on down.
jokes.setup: Taking photos of Bob Barker's house
jokes.punchline: Will imagines Bob Barker being sexual and references his game show catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Clever reference humor mixing The Price Is Right with adult content - the 'Bobby' nickname adds charm

jokes.text: Look at Richard Simmons in his back yard cooking them big old stank sausages.
jokes.setup: Will describes another celebrity photo
jokes.punchline: The visual of Richard Simmons cooking smelly sausages
jokes.explanation: Funny visual incongruity of fitness guru Richard Simmons with unhealthy food

jokes.text: You're not letting your cerebellum rotate.
jokes.setup: Publisher doesn't understand Will's artistic vision
jokes.punchline: Will uses completely wrong anatomical term for thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect malapropism showing Will's pseudo-intellectual pretensions while revealing his actual knowledge level

jokes.text: I'm ambidextrous.
jokes.setup: Will explains he's both a photographer and writer
jokes.punchline: Uses 'ambidextrous' instead of 'versatile' or 'multi-talented'
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Will malapropism - confusing physical dexterity with creative versatility

jokes.text: Number four: Get naked. What? WILL: No, my fault. I was just kidding.
jokes.setup: Romantic recorded instructions for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate instruction followed by immediate backpedal
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom sex joke with appropriate backtrack - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Good evening, you fine Nubian princess. I set up a little surprise for you tonight, so just follow instructions and enjoy.
jokes.setup: Will setting up romantic surprise via recording
jokes.punchline: Will's over-the-top romantic language and controlling tone
jokes.explanation: 'Nubian princess' is classic Will romantic cheese. Sets up the elaborate surprise sequence.

jokes.text: When there's a sock in the door, you sleep on the lawn.
jokes.setup: Carlton interrupts Will's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Reference to established roommate protocol
jokes.explanation: Good world-building detail that explains Carlton's intrusion with established rules

jokes.text: 'Jay Leno: Earth Enemy Number One' by Will Smith?
jokes.setup: Will's innocent photo being published
jokes.punchline: The completely over-the-top headline attributing environmental destruction to Jay Leno
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from coffee photo to environmental catastrophe - the hyperbolic headline is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Well, at least I got paid.
jokes.setup: Learning he's been tricked and Leno is being defamed
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate focus on the positive financial aspect
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Will's self-centered priorities even in crisis

jokes.text: Jay Leno is suing me for $10 million.
jokes.setup: The tabloid story consequences
jokes.punchline: Massive lawsuit amount for ridiculous situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation beat that raises stakes to ridiculous levels

jokes.text: How do you go from trying to make a little impression on Lisa... to being sued by Jay Leno for $10 million? / Um... I'm goofy?
jokes.setup: Phil's exasperated summary of the situation
jokes.punchline: Will offering 'goofy' as explanation for the entire catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that ties the whole episode together. 'Goofy' as catch-all explanation is perfect.

jokes.text: Um... I'm goofy?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks how this situation happened
jokes.punchline: Will sheepishly references the episode's central running gag
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that ties the episode's theme together while showing Will's self-awareness

jokes.text: I mean, he like Hugh Hefner. And I'm an innocent young girl from the Midwest.
jokes.setup: Will explaining how the publisher tricked him
jokes.punchline: Will casts himself as innocent Midwestern girl to Hugh Hefner
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comparison - Will Smith as naive Midwestern girl is perfectly unexpected and funny

jokes.text: I mean, he like Hugh Hefner. And I'm an innocent young girl from the Midwest.
jokes.setup: Will claiming to be the victim
jokes.punchline: Will comparing himself to a naive farm girl being exploited by a porn mogul
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comparison. Will as 'innocent farm girl' is hilariously wrong on every level.

jokes.text: I do something stupid, you clean it all up. The only difference is this time, I didn't do nothing.
jokes.setup: Will acknowledging the pattern of Uncle Phil helping him
jokes.punchline: Will's claim of innocence despite obvious guilt
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness mixed with denial. Will understanding the pattern but claiming exception.

jokes.text: I'd pay $10 million to not have to hear that again. / I'd pay $10 million so I didn't have to say it again.
jokes.setup: Will doesn't want to hear Phil's 'there comes a time' speech
jokes.punchline: Both characters would pay the lawsuit amount to avoid the cliche
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-callback where characters acknowledge sitcom cliches while referencing the episode's stakes

jokes.text: Hilary's elaborate maid route explanation for cocktail sauce spill
jokes.setup: Ashley asks what's wrong with the house
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific and impractical concern about maid efficiency during parties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - the ultra-specific rich person problem with named maid shows her complete disconnect

jokes.text: You're right. Who cares how far Hildegard has to walk?
jokes.setup: Ashley says her maid concern is ridiculous
jokes.punchline: Hilary agrees but misses the point entirely - still focused on maid's convenience
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Hilary's complete lack of self-awareness about her privilege

jokes.text: destroying the rain forest, the ozone layer... and the one tree that grows in Brooklyn.
jokes.setup: Listing Jay Leno's alleged environmental crimes
jokes.punchline: Escalates from global destruction to one specific tree reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation ending with specific literary reference to 'A Tree Grows in Brooklyn' - perfect mix of scope

jokes.text: You're liable for libel. Get it? Those are homonyms.
jokes.setup: Phil explains Will is legally liable
jokes.punchline: Carlton makes word play and proudly explains it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - clever wordplay delivered with characteristic pretension and explanation

jokes.text: Carlton, your next crack is gonna be in your head.
jokes.setup: Carlton makes bad pun during Will's crisis
jokes.punchline: Will threatens violence with his own wordplay on 'crack'
jokes.explanation: Good comeback using Carlton's own wordplay format against him

jokes.text: Listen, John. How long have you been a standup? / About 10 years. Why? / I just hate this part of my job. / What part? / Dude, you've been bumped.
jokes.setup: Will asking about comedian's experience
jokes.punchline: Will revealing he's bumping the veteran comedian from the show
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and role reversal. The veteran getting bumped by the troublemaker is ironic.

jokes.text: I won't even pick the cotton out of a bottle of aspirin.
jokes.setup: Comedian's bit about being 'pro-black'
jokes.punchline: Refusing to interact with cotton due to slavery associations
jokes.explanation: Topical racial humor that works within the show's context but feels borrowed rather than original

jokes.text: I think I did you a favor, bro. Come on.
jokes.setup: Comedian sharing his best material
jokes.punchline: Will dismissing quality comedy as doing him a favor by not letting him perform it
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's poor judgment about comedy while maintaining his confidence.

jokes.text: But I couldn't take it because I'm black. What I mean is, you know, the bottle had cotton in it, you know. Get it? Cotton. I'm black.
jokes.setup: Will stealing the comedian's joke
jokes.punchline: Will butchers the delivery and over-explains the punchline
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy showing how stolen material fails when delivered poorly - the over-explanation kills it

jokes.text: You, goofy? Gee, I can't imagine how she got that idea.
jokes.setup: Will explains he was trying to prove he wasn't goofy
jokes.punchline: Jay sarcastically comments while Will is literally crashing a TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect situational irony - Jay's sarcasm lands because Will is proving Lisa right in real time

jokes.text: You are goofy, and you are an idiot. That's beside the point. It's kind of a romantic thing.
jokes.setup: Jay acknowledging Will's flaws
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismissing those flaws to focus on the romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction structure with Jay's talk show host diplomacy smoothing over the insults

jokes.text: I'm saying... What do you mean by 'wacky'?
jokes.setup: Lisa calls Will 'wacky' after his TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Will immediately gets defensive about another synonym for goofy
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback showing Will learned nothing - still defensive about synonyms for his core trait

jokes.text: I'm saying... What do you mean by 'wacky'?
jokes.setup: Lisa calling Will 'wacky' after his TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Will immediately getting defensive about another synonym for goofy
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending. Will hasn't learned anything and is still sensitive about his image.

jokes.text: No, he's not wacky. No. / She did not mean that. / No, I mean, you're an irresponsible lazy bum, but you're not...
jokes.setup: Family defending Will from being called wacky
jokes.punchline: Carlton's defense that includes calling him an irresponsible lazy bum
jokes.explanation: Great ending callback. Carlton's attempt to defend Will actually makes it worse.
